mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, reinforcing corporate earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition yields positive 20-day returns in over 50% of historical instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising new home sales combined with expanding retail sales indicate strong consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which directly boosts equity valuations and yields positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle peak signals capacity constraints and rising input costs that erode profit margins, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.03 over any 36-month rolling window.
